text: Hello, this is a case of laparoscopic appendicitomy. This patient has the previous two episodes of the acute appendicitis and right now she is in little interval state.

The surgeon uses a Striker mini alligator to grasp the appendix and major appendix together. The harmonic device is then used to separate the appendix from the major appendix. Adhesions from the paracolic gutter and peritoneum are visible, and the mini alligator effectively holds the appendix, allowing for dissection without an additional port. Medial traction is applied to mobilize the appendix towards its base.

The surgeon uses an extracorporeal Melger knot to secure the base of the appendix, which has been skeletonized. The striker mini alligator is introduced into the loop to grasp the appendix for removal.

The surgeon uses a knot pusher to guide the extracorporeal knot towards the base of the appendix, near the cecum. The knot is tightened securely around the appendix base, ensuring it shrinks at the cecum. The knot is tightened multiple times for security.
The surgeon completes the appendectomy by cutting the appendix with harmonic scissors, which seals the distal portion and cauterizes the mucosa to prevent future mucosal issues.

The surgeon uses a striker mini alligator through a five millimeter left iliac fossa port to perform a laparoscopic appendectomy. After dissection, a five millimeter telescope is used to visualize and pull the appendix through the umbilicus, with reduced vision noted during the maneuver.

The surgeon carefully pulls the appendix out through the cannula, ensuring it remains hidden within the cannula to minimize exposure. An endo bag is used to further reduce the risk of infection as the appendix is extracted from the abdominal cavity.
